I ve been a Mint Mobile customer since around 2021, and while the price is cheap, there is absolutely no benefit to being a loyal customer.I understand Mint s whole concept is low-cost service, but after staying with the company for years, you would think they would offer something to reward long-term customers. Instead, the only thing I ve ever received is a small holiday card in the mail.The service itself works fine most of the time, but the complete lack of loyalty perks is disappointing. No discounts, no upgrades, no real incentives to stay.At this point, I m honestly considering going back to a traditional phone carrier that at least offers rewards, promotions, or appreciation for customers who stay long-term.Mint Mobile may be cheap, but loyalty clearly doesn t mean much here.

The cell service is great -I love it! It is the customer service / policies which do not work well. I received a credit card statement with a charge from Mint Mobile. I paid for a full year, and I am not due until Sept. So, I called what is this charge. I was told I increased from 5G to 20G!!! I told the CS person I did not sign up for 20G I do not even use the 5G. She said I had 7 days to cancel and get a refund and that time is up. I told her if I knew about this I would have cancelled - I never received an email from MM for the updated service or a notice of xxxx being charged to my credit card. She gave me 2 options she would put a credit into my account (wallet) for use later or I can suspend my service and get a refund. I do not want to suspend my service - I asked her if I do not renew my services in Sept what happens to the credit - it stays in my account until I use it, I will not get a refund to my credit card. I told her I wanted to talk to someone else and that ended up being the accounting dept. I want a logical explanation how this happened.  There is none and accounting can do nothing to help me. I was also told the credit mentioned earlier is no longer available - my account was marked "denied" like in I did not want the credit offered. I told the accounting dept this is false. The take aways are 1) they have no policy or way to change my 20G back to 5G until my Sept due date. 2) They have no policy or person in the entire company who can reverse the denied credit to credit - even if this was their fault. 3) they have no policy to issue a refund unless you cancel a service within 7 days or suspend your cell service with them.It takes a lot of effort on Mint Mobile's part to copy and paste the script they send everyone who has come across poor customer service. It would be refreshing to see "I am the manager of Mint Mobile my name is (insert a name) I can help you". I do not want to hear another I apology we have no one in the entire company who can undo the deny as in I did not want my money. Even if our supervisor errored because she did not understand English. I apologize I do not understand how you got 20G service - I believe you when you say did not want it or need it. I apologize - I hear your frustration - but I cannot do anything to help you.

I was with Mint for 5 years.In the first 3 or 4 years during summer vacation season at the east coast I did accept having more often than not zero data service despite good or even excellent radio coverage/conditions (full bars). The data service at the coast was much better outside of that busy season. This is presumably because Mint Mobile customers have low QoS / priority than T-Mobile customers who paid a higher fee.But since 1-2 years, I and my wife have both been observing this low-to-no rate data services at many other places including in our town, and frequently. We suspect that this has to do with T-Mobile acquiring Mint. Mint s customers have presumably been either further deprioritized, or T-Mobile s network capacity doesn t keep up with demand.When I called Mint explaining the problem and my suspicion, the answer was  Mint s customers are not on a lower data service priority than T-Mobile customers . Well, I m from this field and know that prioritizing subscribers is a basic feature of telecommunications networks, so I don t buy it.Anyway, because problem was not gone, my wife and I ended up switching to a low cost prepaid plan with AT&T. What a difference: problem gone. We get decent data service everywhere!

I switched from Verizon to Mint Mobile over a year ago for better value, and while the network hasn t been perfect, it s been good overall. Calls generally work fine and day-to-day service has been acceptable, which is why this whole situation has been so frustrating.Since October 30, 2025, I ve been unable to purchase a replacement phone as an existing customer due to what appears to be a checkout issue on Mint s website. Every attempt to buy a device-only phone fails during checkout and the order gets auto-canceled or auto-refunded. I ve tried multiple browsers, multiple payment methods (credit cards and PayPal), and confirmed with my bank that nothing is being declined. The problem is clearly on Mint s side.I ve spent well over 12 hours on calls and chats with support, including Level 2 and internal escalation. I was promised a callback that never happened, and I ve been given different explanations depending on who I talked to, including being told it was just an address issue, which doesn t line up with the later failed orders using correct information.Because Mint won t allow me to complete the purchase at the advertised sale price, I m now stuck using an older phone that doesn t support 5G. That has directly hurt my coverage and reliability where I live, and it shouldn t be this hard to simply replace a phone.I ve filed FCC and BBB complaints, but I d still rather just hear from someone at Mint who has the authority to actually fix this. I chose Mint over Verizon for a reason, and I want to stay   but customer service and purchasing need serious improvement.Update: Mint Mobile responded publicly asking me to call customer service again. I ve already spent over 12 hours on calls with that same number, including Level 2 support and internal escalation, without resolution. Redirecting me back to the same support loop does not address the issue. I m still waiting for contact from someone with the authority to resolve the existing-customer checkout block.

I m sharing this experience to help others avoid issues with Mint Mobile s referral program.If you plan to refer a friend for the advertised $45 referral credit, be aware that using the 15NOW promo code included in the referral email can invalidate the referral bonus, even though this is not clearly explained.Here s what happened. My husband referred me through his Mint account using Refer a Friend   Share by email. The referral email contained a default message stating that I could use the code 15NOW to get $15 off a 3-month plan, plus $15 in renewal credit. I clicked the referral link in that email and entered the code during checkout, unaware that this code is a separate promotion that cancels the referral credit (I would get $15, but my husband would not receive the $45 referral bonus).Mint s email states that referral credits may take up to two weeks to appear. After waiting the full two weeks and seeing no credit in my husband s account, we contacted customer support.My husband first used online chat. The representative said there was no referral record and suggested the referral may not have been completed correctly. They also mentioned this was a common issue, which raises concerns about why the system or messaging hasn t been improved.I then called customer support. The first representative verified both accounts and my purchase order number and confirmed that my husband s account was not linked to my purchase. When I asked whether any credit could be issued, the call was disconnected.I called again and went through the full verification process a second time. After a long hold, I was told again that no referral was visible and that because the credit should have appeared by now, they could not do anything without proof. I was advised to submit screenshots via chat.I uploaded screenshots of the referral email and purchase confirmation. The chat experience was inconsistent and confusing: I was first told to wait another day for the credit to show up in my husband's account, then told a  special offer  was applied, then given incorrect information about referral amounts. After asking for clarification and references, the representative eventually acknowledged that the referral bonus should be $45 but stated that using the 15NOW code invalidated it. This was frustrating because the code appears automatically in the default referral email text generated by Mint s own system.After nearly four hours across phone and chat support, Mint ultimately issued $20 in credits, which does not match the advertised referral bonus and felt like a partial workaround rather than a resolution.Conclusion:1. Customer service representatives need clearer training and more consistent information. Repeating the same explanations across multiple representatives for hours, and being disconnected mid-call, is not acceptable.2. The referral program is misleading. If using the 15NOW code cancels the $45 referral bonus, this should be clearly stated in the referral email or on the referral page. Customers should not be penalized for using a default message generated by Mint s own system.I hope Mint improves both the clarity of its referral program and the quality of its customer support.

I explicitly did not authorize any additional purchases.The Shocking Result:When provided a new eSim, installed and working, I thanked the rep and disconnected. Immediately after the call ended, I was hit with unauthorized charges:$86.31 for a Mint Mobile Protection Plan I never requested$24.63 for a 10-Day International Pass I never authorizedAn additional attempted charge of $388.45 (which my card company blocked and I declined)Mint Mobile's Response:When I requested a full refund and account cancellation, Mint Mobile refused, claiming I was "outside of their activation policy of 7 days." This makes absolutely no sense since I received the phone on January 30th and activated it on January 31st - that's ONE DAY and less than 60 minutes of activation time. I made zero calls on the device and used zero data.Their "thorough consideration" apparently didn't include actually reading my complaint or checking the timeline. The policy they cited doesn't even apply to my situation.The Bottom Line:What should have been a simple trial turned into unauthorized charges and a complete refusal to make things right. This experience demonstrated exactly why I should NOT move my family's phone service to Mint Mobile.If a company adds unauthorized charges during a support call and then refuses refunds based on policies that don't apply, how can you trust them with your ongoing service?I'm now filing complaints with the FTC, Tennessee Division of Consumer Affairs, and Better Business Bureau. Save yourself the headache and choose a more reputable provider.** UPDATE 2/4/2026 **I received an email update that my refund has been approved, which also provided a return shipping label.  I was advised it will take up to 10 business days to process the refund once the phone has been received. I'll post another update when I receive the refund.

My experience with Mint Mobile over the past year has been great! I wasn t expecting a Trust Pilot score so low fr. Anyway, this is my second time with them. The first time I paid them (last year) I paid the each $45 for three months for two phones. I renewed the three month plan once and had to cancel because my son decided to go to college in Alaska and Mint Mobile does not work out there. We switched to Verizon in August and I went back to paying $180 a month for two phones which does not include a monthly payment for the phones because I own the phones outright. I as upset at having to pay so much again for the same service but Verizon & ATT are the only services that reach Alaska and Mint runs off of TMobile towers.I caved in November, I couldn t keep paying Verizon that much every month. I took my phone off of the Verizon account and came back to Mint Mobile. I was so sure about the service that I paid for the full year on the Unlimited Plan at a discount of $180 ($206 after taxes). It feels AMAZING to pay $200 and have no phone bill for the rest of the year. One month of Verizon took care of me for 12 months at Mint. Who can beat that? Even at the renewal price of $360 that s worlds better than paying the big companies more money for what s essentially the same service. I still pay Verizon $100 a month for my sons service and unfortunately I ll have to do that until he graduates   Once he does finish school he s going to have to pay for himself if he decides to stay in Alaska.I did read through some of the bad reviews and while agree that it s annoying to have to deal with foreign customer service with a heavy accent (I really wish larger companies would stop doing that) I understand that Mint Mobile is cheap, and to stay cheap they probably have to pay employees cheaper than what s reasonable in America. When you pay your bill anywhere else you re essentially paying for EVERYTHING, including CS quality, app features, UI, etc. I do find it annoying that you can t simply have a family plan meshed into one account like other phone service companies but figuring out how to link other ppl to your account was simple enough. When I had my son on the plan it took me all of 10 mins to get everything paid for and connected.Also keep in mind the Mint Mobile runs off of T-Mobile towers. I live in a major city so my service is fine. If you re in a rural area you re going to get just about as good of service that T-Mobile would get, I suggest Visible instead of Mint, they run off of Verizon Towers. (I d switch my son to Visible but others on Reddit have suggested against it) I may still try it though because $100 a month for ONE PAID OFF PHONE is just ridiculous. If I stay with Verizon it would ONLY be because I m not going to leave my kid in Alaska with shoddy cell phone service. I ve also been out of the country, to London, and the Minternational plan worked flawlessly. I just connected to the hotel WiFi and when I was out I used data for GPS out googling places to go instead of streaming, scrolling or video calling while I was out. Those are hotel WiFi activities lol. Besides I wasn t about to have my face buried in my phone while I out anyway, I wanted to enjoy the city.Mint makes it easy to do most things you need yourself in the app. The one time I had to call CS was to cancel to switch to Verizon. So if you re someone who needs a lot of help maybe consider other companies. I recommended my mom to Mint, she signed up without my help in December, paid for the year and she s had zero complaints. The whole family is about to be on Mint lol.

Mint advertises things like  we ve lowered our prices again,  but in reality these reductions apply only to new customers. Renewal prices are noticeably higher, which isn t made clear in the marketing.When you select a plan, the price displayed during browsing does not include an added  recovery fee  that only appears at checkout. This makes the quoted prices inaccurate and makes comparison shopping difficult.Customer support is another pain point. Their chatbot will acknowledge when an issue requires a human agent, but repeatedly tries to redirect you instead of connecting you. This feels like an intentional design choice that works against customers trying to resolve real problems.There are also technical issues:  If your plan has expired, the system will still send 2-factor codes to the inactive number, preventing you from logging in to renew.  Their phone support line disconnects international callers immediately, making it impossible to renew by phone while traveling.Overall, Mint Mobile has potential, but the combination of unclear pricing, support obstacles, and technical problems made it a frustrating experience for me.

If you just need cell phone service to make calls, Mint might be worth it. However, if you want to send and receive texts the same day, or want to be able to connect to the internet, browse, shop, etc. go somewhere else.Texts are received one to two days later than they are sent and THERE IS NO CELLULAR DATA.Sure, you're paying for data. You might even be paying for "unlimited data". But there is none,They advertise that they use T-Mobile towers, and they knowingly and dishonestly give prospective customers the false impression that you will receive the same service as T-Mobile customers,but they don't tell you that Mint customers have the absolute lowest priority for cellular data bandwidth thrugh those towers.Bandwidth is limited and T-Mobile customers have higher priority to use that bandwidth than Mint and other MVNO's.So, 99% of the time, you are out of luck. There is no cellular data left over for the lowest priority users like Mint customers. If you luck out and are able to connect, it will be slow and it will not last long.So, if all you need is make phone calls, Mint might be for you. Otherwise, look somewhere else. On August 14, 2025, we signed up online, paid $624.69 via our credit card, and got Mint mobile contract for three lines. We were supposed to get instructions, within a couple of hours, from Mint on how to transfer our phones over to Mint by eSim.  We waited and did not get the instructions. We called Mint customer service the next day and Mint decided to cancel the first order and set up a second order because they didn't have a record of the first order being paid for. Mint did so and charged our credit card a second time. We were instructed to call our credit card company and dispute the charges for the first order. We called the CC company only to find out that the first charge never went through.  After a couple of days, the first charge finally appeared on our credit card. We disputed the charge, and everything seemed fine. We received emails to add the eSims to our phones and it looked like all was well.  On Oct 27, two of the three phone lines were deactivated by Mint, claiming we needed to send them payment to get them reactivated.  Come to find out that the two deactivated lines had been attached to the first contract that got cancelled, even though the second contract (the good one) shows all three lines, including the two that got deactivated.  We spent the next 5 days talking to numerous customer service agents, trying to get them to understand the problem and get the phones reactivated.  We finally got the phones reactivated on Oct 31, only after they told us we had to pay again (paying again was "part of the process"). This time we had to pay two separate charges of $512.58 and $133.36. We have now paid double for one contract. Also, Mint only gives you 45 days to dispute the charges or you get an email saying it's past the 45 days to get your money back. As of 11/20, Mint has not refunded those two charges.  Now we are fighting with them again. Half the time we can't understand what they are saying because Mint's customer service is made up of people from foreign countries that do not speak English very well.  All we want is the money back that is ours and the phones to stay on.  Is this too much to ask?  If this can't be resolved we will go to the BBB, FCC, the State Attorney General and the news media.I saw you reply:It's not going to do any good to call Customer Service because we have probably talked to 30-40 people who have all said they have taken care of it.  That is totally a lie.  We have been fighting for the duplicate charge to be refunded since November 3, 2025 since it was charged to our Credit Card.  How will this change anything now?

I was forced to end the family plan, help my daughter cancel auto-renew through a text Mint sent HER, and dig deep into hidden settings to disable auto-renew on my own line. By contrast, enabling auto-renew is easy and front-and-center on the dashboard, but disabling it is deliberately buried.I also wanted to keep one child s line active for just one more month (since he was joining us abroad later), but Mint offered no option for a one-month service   only longer auto-renewals.This is manipulative and unfair to customers. It should not take multiple steps, lost parental controls, and support intervention just to turn off auto-renew.The only reason this is 2 stars is because the service itself worked fine. Not as cheap as they let on in the ads, but it was good enough service. But ending it is time-consuming and so frustrating.All that said, the hassle of turning off auto-renew is reason enough for me to never use Mint again.

Warehouse manager switching providers. Lined my purchase up with my monthly payment with my current provider to avoid paying two providers for the month.Mint mobile used Fed-Ex as a 3rd party shipper. Tracking showed the package at local facility early morning and a delivery time of 8am-12:30pm.As a manager, not having a phone for a few hours is ok but the entire day is unacceptable.When Mint sends your package via Fed-Ex, they are offered options to allow the buyer to pick up the package in case of a delay or anything along the lines. I know this because I do shipping and receiving via Fed-Ex in my position. Mint does NOT choose this option.Which would be fine. Except, when the buyer calls Fed-Ex with receipts and tracking numbers to pick up a package at their facilty, of immediate necessity, Fed-Ex needs permission from the shipper to allow the buyer to pick it up at said location. Problem is, Mint mobile not only refuses to contact Fed-Ex for their 'new customer', citing policy, but they were caught flat out lying about their policy.  On one hand this couldn't be done because lines were busy, and a 3-way call was not feasible; even though this was suggested by a Mint agent himself, not me, apparently this was against policy. Even though he initiated the idea and made the 3-way call but did not follow through with Fed-Ex agent.I have the entire thing recorded. If you are a company involving a 3rd party to complete a contract you signed up for, but are unwilling to contact the 3rd party of your choice when that 3rd party doesn't complete the delivery on time, holding up their end, how do you then have a policy as unethical as holding your customer hostage for a product they have already paid for and supposedly have no wiggle room to contact the company you chose as a 3rd party venture? New guys on the road cross-state, after hours and no contact if it were needed. But it shows why they lock you up in 3 month and yearly contracts. At least Mint mobile has  made its money.. and at that point, customer service is out of the window.

I've used Mint Mobile for a year now.I love the transparency about cost. Companies are supposed to tell potential customers the cost for their services, and all too often, when it comes to phone/internet/cable providers, even such simple transparency is difficult to come by. Mint tells you what your cost for the whole year will be, clearly, up front, and without making you get halfway through signing up first.Personally, I'm the sort of person who loves to be able to sign up for a whole year of service at a good price, pay up front, and then not worry about it until it's time to pay again.I don't use much data, so I pay $180 a year for the cheapest plan. If my usage habits change in the future, I will be able to simply pay the difference in price between my current plan and the cost of the next level up in order to upgrade. (After using Mint for a year, this has so far never happened.)Signing up is easy and simple. The app is easy to use and understand. And renewing my plan for another year was easy as well.As for my service coverage, it seems quite similar to the coverage my friends and co-workers have, but they pay a lot more each month than I do. (Admittedly, I live in a semi-rural area, so there are occasionally dead zones, but those are dead zones for everyone, and the folks with the big name carriers also don't get signal in those spots, so it's not really a point against Mint.)

Mint Mobile's unlimited high speed data offer is a scam. They offer unlimited talk, text, and high speed mobile data, but they make a concerted effort to barely inform you of a 40 gig cap on the high speed data. If you do not have home internet, or in my case simply did not connect your device to the wifi as the advent of unlimited mobile data offerings from ALL carriers gave me as it turns out a false sense of security. That 40 gigs of data will get you about 10-15 days of normal use, then without warning your mobile internet becomes bricked. Litterly you cannot load any URL, and all app internet functionality is gone, you can't even use the Mint Mobile app for support. Mint Mobile's terms of service state when data cap is realized your mobile data queries will lose priority over all others during times of network congestion, but the data throttling they unleash on you will last for hours in duration, with longer periods over night, at 3-4-5AM, in my small town. There is no way their network is congested at 4AM. They offer high speed data boosts, $10 for 5gb, and $20 for 10gb, and while you are pondering on and realizing what a bad deal that is for you as even the 10gb won't get you through until the monthly resetting of the cap, just then they offer you a previously not mentioned "Unnecessary Plan" with a 60gb high speed data cap, only this is a line change and the $15 a month initial enrollment deal that even brought you to Mint Mobile in the first place no longer applies. You are also well beyond their guaranteed 7 days no questions asked return and refund policy, so despite the 3 month premium you just paid 14 days prior, you now must pay 3 months of the $60 per month premium in order to even start the new 60 gb high speed data capped plan, so that means you're out $225 within the first 2 weeks for a functional modern cell phone. Mint mobile sucks you in with a great price on an Unlimited Data Plan, and bait and switches ya with a low high speed mobile data cap, gives you litterly no bandwith in which to even remotely use mobile internet data upon reaching that cap, and then demands way more money to restore functionality. Pretty good scheme in all honesty, any fines ever imposed for such wanton money bilking and conniving thievery will pale in comparison to the profits such large-scale skulduggery can generate. UPDATE  I have now seen my mobile data throttled to the point that without wifi I can only make calls and send text messages. Any and all mobile data functionality is gone, and I have learned Mint Mobile's terms of service is a bunch of lies. After chatting with an employee I come to find out that losing priority for your mobile data queries during times of network congestion is supplemental to being throttled down to 512 kbps or 2G. Well, I have been doing speed tests when I can actually get them to work and the top speed I have seen is 0.29mbps, or 112kbps. The average I have been getting is 0.04 mbps or 40 kbps. 40kbps is cartoonishly slow, I used to get 52 kbps on the Earthlink dial-up free trial about 20 years ago. I cannot fathom how Mint Mobile is getting away with this blatant ripoff "unlimited" plan that is NOT unlimited. I think I'm at the file a complaint with the Federal Trade Commision, and the PA Attorney General's Bureau Of Consumer Protection point in this journey, and I will be porting my number to another carrier ASAP. Before you leave your existing carrier from being enticed by Mint Mobiles promises to provide comparable service for a bargain price, know that they are full of it. They offer comparable service as long as you keep ponying up money, and you will be very disappointed in the end.

When I moved to the US from Canada, it was supposed to be temporary (~2 yrs) so knew I would want a US number while in US. I came across Mint and decided to support a fellow Canuck (not that he needs my money). The price is hard to beat given how we are ripped off in Canada with the big three monopoly.NOTES:+/- Website was not great then, but has improved since... some.- Website:  most annoying is how information on use is displayed, but that seems to now be gone.+ One thing they got rid of was separation of topping up for data vs roaming by merging into one wallet. That gives you the ability to use your money however you needed.+ I like that each month, I get a heads up when I start a new month. I also get a reminder (text & email) when invoicing is coming up. This is incredibly appreciated as payment is for a full year if you want to get the best price.(For those that say they find out after they are billed, check your account settings. You can turn off 'auto-renewal'; this is nowhere buried in fine print but in full size print - read the page. I always review all website account settings for just such settings and to minimize spam.)- Customer Service:  hours aren't great (not 24/7) and are Pacific time. Only bot is 24/7 and it isn't great, esp if don't use correct words. (Yes, it's still early for such technology.)---! One thing that never worked? Data roaming in Canada. Baffling as calling Canada (yes, from US) is included in the plans (as well as Mexico). I called and never got it fixed; one time making sure a technical support ticket was logged so it could be investigated.On this trip to Canada, I was thrilled to learn of Mint's "free Canada roaming" - perfect!! I don't need a local number/sim!! WRONG! Data roaming still didn't work.Worse, customer support - sorry, Mint (not sorry) - sucked. First call:  "disconnected" while I was waiting for them to confirm local carrier. Second call:  she refused to answer same question without all my account information, which wasn't needed to answer my question. Third call:  she said she'd checked who it was (without my account info), and then pretended to put me on hold. After 8 minutes, I put myself on mute. 10 seconds later, she pretended to rejoin the call, saying "Hello? Hello?" and ignoring anything I said. She hung up. Not impressed.--> Canada roaming solution:  After doing some more extensive research, I landed on this page about setting up an APN manually. In the past, this has not worked. This one did so sharing:  androidauthority's site (dot com/p-3497632/).I can't believe that after 4 yrs of roaming not working, this is the only way to get it working - but at least it's working. I may be keeping an eye out for other options as I will be staying a few more years in the US.

If I could give this company a negative score I would!They're advertisement of a 3mo/$45 plan and promises of being on one of the fastest networks sounded great! I went online to sign up for an eSim (as the website stated my phone would be compatible). 4 days later my phone failed to make/receive calls, texts, and would not load anything needing the internet unless I switched to WiFi. After literally 10hrs of trying to get it fixed and being disconnected from online live support agents, I was able to reach an agent who told me my phone was in fact NOT compatible with eSim and needed a physical sim card! Question 1. Why did their system approve the eSim transaction and process if my phone is not compatible?! Thankfully the agent refunded my transaction, assured me my sim card could be overnighted, and told me she'd put me through to the sales team to get a physical sim card. The sales agent came online and told me I needed to purchase a plan (Q 2. Why refund me if I need to repurchase?) and that I would have to pay $75 because I wasn't considered a new customer anymore! Cordially I contested that I was, in fact, a new cuatomer, relayed to her what happened, and requested the price I paid to begin with. After about 20 minutes of this, I requested to speak to a manager. The rep became combative, stating their manager would say the same thing, stated there was no manager to transfer to, and argued with me. She then proceeded to tell me that she processed the transaction (WITHOUT my approval). At this point, I told her I did not want to work with her anymore and to please have a manager call me on my boyfriend's phone. About 25-30 minutes later I received a call from the same rep I had just told I did not want to deal with..they then transferred me to her supervisor. I proceeded to tell her all that had happened (for the third time), also relaying that the first rep assured me the sim card would be overnighted. The supervisor told me that they couldn't overnight the card and said I would receive it in 2 business days OR 3-5 business days. I asked why I couldnt get it overnighted and about  the confusion on the multiple shipping days and for either I didn't receive a  straight answer. I suspected I wasn't speaking to a supervisor so when I requested some type of proof that I was speaking to a supervisor, (I asked for an email with her credentials, a phone number with her diredirect extension, etc), she did not have any way of providing such information- leading me to believe the rep previously simply asked her call center neighbor to make the call to me. I say this because even when asking for a possible extension for her she replied, confused, "An extension? What's that?" To me, any one working in a call center knows what that is...ESPECIALLY a Supervisor. I did get her name at least. By the end of the call, I had to pay the $75 and was still told my card would be there in 2 days or 3-5 business days. This all happened December 14thToday is Christmas eve, 10 days later. When I first sign up it was for the unlimited plan and when they resigned me, thought I was on the same one that I originally paid for. Well today my service was snail slow and after a while I finally looked into the plan I have and was shocked to find out I have the 5gb plan and had to pay an extra $20 for 3TBS!I'm disgusted! Between the lack of professionalism, the worst customer service I've ever experienced (toward a new customer at that!), the lack of clear communication, the spotty service, and unfulfilled promises I am fed up!I hope this saves EVERYONE from signing up with Mint Mobile! Save your money, time, and sanity!

No order number, and they keep on asking me for the tracking number. How am I supposed to have a tracking number? I never got anything via email or over the phone. Now the 7-day trial is running out of time (tomorrow), and is already out of text messages. Monday evening, a CS rep stated that he would add more text messages to the SIM. another lie. No text messaging possible. I requested a physical address, so I can send a letter via snail mail and inform the corporate office about what's going on. And now I am trying to get my customer number and PIN for the two of us via phone, and I have been on hold for exactly 14:35 minutes right now. It is 6:24 p.m. I bet you they are having me on hold until 7:00 p.m. when they are officially closed for the day. Terrible company now. It used to be fine, but no more.12/12/2024 update: Since I don't trust anymore what the Mint CS reps over in India or wherever they may be say or put in their chat, I followed up this morning to see if the requested SIM card (initially requested on 12/7, promised to arrive no later than 12/10 or 12/11) indeed had shipped overnight as requested last night. And what do you know... nope. Order number was incomplete, so nothing moved along. I was on the phone for another 25 minutes with yet another CS rep who issued me the correct (hopefully) order number and said something about putting a note on my account to ship the SIM as soon as possible. Oh no.... I told him to put in to ship IMMEDIATELY and to request overnight shipment. And instead "I will add that note" and then never do it after disconnecting the call, I told him I would hold so he could put that note on the account right now, right this minute. I received an email a couple hours later that the SIM has shipped, but no tracking number. Also no clue how it shipped. For all I know, it can be another week for it to arrive. tracking[at]mintmobile.com also is not a valid email address, so there is no way to confirm any shipment or the speed thereof. CS reps at Mint tell customers to wait for the tracking number and THEN after the fact to call to request faster shipping. That is absolute B.S. The shipping speed will be entered first and then a tracking number is issued. So asking customers to call after the tracking number was issued, is just mocking them and stringing them along. I absolutely HATE when companies play those shady games with their customers. Maybe it's time to bring back customer service to the country in which the customers actually reside.And for the record, Mint sent a canned reply to my review, and they ask me to call or chat to resolve the issues. Isn't that too funny? Can't make this stuff up. So much ignorance...

Mint canceled my service during a catastrophe. I'd been with Mint over a year (maybe 2) and Hurricane Milton came through my town on 10-9-24 and my annual billing was 10-12-24 (Saturday). The hurricane's eye went over my city, taking out cell towers, internet and electric. On Sunday 10-13-24, I drove to another city and found I had no service. I called Mint and they said my payment had been declined; the same method I had used before which was good. They ended up trying 4 good credit cards and every one of them were declined.I asked Mint if they would connect my service for 2 days so I could sort this out and contact my family so they would know I was okay. Mint said no. I asked if they could call my credit card company for me and they did call them and transferred me. The card company said there was no reason for the card to be declined. I called Mint back (the only connection I had with my phone) and they tried my card again and it was declined again. I explained the hurricane damage, the cell towers that were out in my city and asked if they would give me one business day to figure this out. Mint said no.I went to Target in that city and signed up with Consumer Cellular. Once I had a working phone, I found an email from my credit card asking me to confirm the charge for the amount Mint charged in a text they sent. Of course, since Mint had disconnected my service, I did not get that text and because I didn't, the card company would not process the charge. Had Mint given me only 1 more hour of service in a city that had cell towers, the charge would have gone through and I would still be with Mint.Maybe they don't have hurricanes whatever country I called. I spoke to no less than 3 or 4 reps and they were not rude, but they were not helpful and lost a long-term customer because they would not help me for 2 days during a natural disaster. I'm paying more, but I talk to reps in the US who I am sure would help me if the situation were in their hands. It is incredible that any company that could, would not help you in a time of dire need. I explained that people died in the storm and my family was unaware of my situation.My family didn't even know if I was alive until I switched my phone service. The phone service was good; the customer service however is a deal-breaker. I lost my number of over 20 years because of Mint, which means there are people who will never be able to locate me again including business associates and I had to notify countless people of the change.Update 12-6-24: Mint responded here asking me to call the customer service department that was the very reason I do not have service with them. What do they expect me to say? They will only respond the same way and say, "I'm sorry, I can't help you with that."

Boy, do I regret it! First of all, process seemed pretty straightforward. However, my digital eSIM card didn t work the first time and I had to go through the process again. Once I finished everything and got my phone working I realized why Mint Mobile is so cheap no service! I gave it three solid days thinking that maybe the phone needed more time to process or if it was just a weird service day. On the fourth day I called customer service to talk to them about this issue thinking it was something on my end. Upon further investigation from representative, she was able to really zoom in on my town and surrounding areas and see that we in fact do not have coverage. Again, The coverage map on the website shows that we have coverage. As I see it, the company lies by saying they have coverage but they do not have it.  The representative was very empathetic, but told me the map online is not a real representation of their coverage.  Due to the time it took me to get back onto my AT&T account, it was over the seven days refund Policy from Mint (by ONE day) And they will not refund me the $150+ I spent for them to lie to me about their service. I would never recommend them and in fact would like to put them on blast deceiving customers. What a disappointment.

So far, I'm not real impressed with Mint. I have been with my current pay-as-you-go carrier for around 25 years. That carrier meets most of my needs and the service has improved steadily. I maintain several lines for relatives and a back-up for me. They don't provide coverage outside the United States (specifically Canada). Mint provides global coverage. $15/month is affordable. Not wanting to make an unnecessary investment, I checked with Mint to make sure one of my phones was compatible. It came back as a "perfect match". I unlocked the phone, purchased 3 months of Mint service and transferred the number. I installed the SIM card when I got it. I discovered that my phone was unable to make or receive calls. I opened a chat with a Mint agent (I don't hear well and accents are particularly difficult for me).The first agent with whom I chatted finally said he had made the necessary adjustments and I needed to check in 15 minutes that my phone was fully functioning. It was not. I opened another chat. I referenced my earlier chat and explained that efforts to restore full functioning had not been successful. This agent determined that my phone had not been "completely" unlocked and referred me back to the carrier. I went back to the carrier and called the unlocking number. After a lot of patient back and forth (my hearing issues), the agent realized that my phone didn't have the capacity necessary to fully activate the SIM card. Although my phone is unable to update it's status or fully engage, it continues to be a "perfect match" for Mint service when I check the web-site. I accepted that my phone was not going to be adequate. Compatible phones on the Mint website are more expensive than similar unlocked phones from other vendors and/or are bundled with plans. (At this point, I am not at all sure I will extend my current Mint plan.) I opened yet another chat with Mint to ask if a phone I had found with another vendor was compatible. The agent would not respond to my question. She told me about the phones available through Mint. I explained why I wanted an alternative and I asked again about the phone in which I was interested. It took at least three attempts to get a straight answer. (I made a copy of that chat session). I am SO glad I didn't attempt this transaction with my primary phone. I rely on that to maintain contact with relatives (I have an elderly sister, a handicapped niece and a brother in fragile health) and it is my contact number for most of the business I do. This was a trial run for Mint, they didn't pass. I am dreading my attempt to fully activate service with a new phone.UPDATE: I received the standard (canned) response from Mint stating that they were "sorry for the inconvenience" and referring me to the chat or customer service support line to "take a look at your case and find the best solution". As I explained in detail, I have already attempted to resolve the issue on at least three chat sessions. I still have a problem. I would like someone at Mint to tell me why my phone was (and is) deemed a perfect match when it is not. I'd like some apology for the problem this has caused me. I am waiting until Prime Day to get a phone that I have been told is compatible in the event that the phone is discounted. If that phone doesn't work out, I will request a refund.

I was a long-time customer of Verizon, but my cel usage had dropped to almost none after I retired. I don't have cel service at my home which is a complicating factor. So I went looking for a less-expensive carrier and saw all the Mint ads on TV. $15 was much better than $60! Then began a nightmare of trying to switch. First problem was my Iphone 6 (which Mint said was compatible) turned out to not be. This wasted 2 weeks. So I bite the bullet and order a new Samsung phone from Mint. The phone shows up, and I spend three fruitless days trying to activate it with no success. I finally get a tech with a smidgin of imagination who connects me with Samsung factory tech (and then drops off the line). The Samsung tech after diagnosing it online, recommends I take it to a Samsung factory service center, which I do the next morning. They pronounce the phone defective and tell me to return it back to Mint for replacement. Some words about Mint's 'customer service' are in order here. One, their techs work off a script. If the script doesn't have an answer, they're lost. If you ask to speak to a supervisor, the 'tech' says they'll 'ask the back room' and they'll get back to you. Figure a minimum of 24 hours for that. Two, every time you call they 'start over' with their script, wasting yet more time. And you won't get the same tech either. Three, their techs have varying degrees of fluency in the English language, surprising considering they're US-based. English is obviously a second language for some. So, back to the defective phone. I call Mint and ask for a return authorization, I'm told this has to go to the 'back room', then they'll get back to me. This has been dragging on for nearly a month at this point. After two days, I call to ask about this. Still no answer. The next day, the 'back room' calls me and says they believe the problem is I don't have cel service at my house, it's not the phone. After I informed them that the attempts were made about 1 block from a T-Mobile store and had plenty of signal AND Samsung itself had declared the phone defective, the 'back room' guy grudgingly admitted they would take it back. 'This may take up to week'. Once the return notice came, they were issuing a REFUND, so I'd have to re-order the phone, yet more delay. This in spite of their website saying they'll REPLACE defective phones. That was the last straw; I said refund the phone and while you're at it, refund my pre-paid plan. I'm going elsewhere. Where did I end up? US mobile (never mentioned in 'best plans'). Not only cheaper (how about $8 per month?) but far superior tech help. They are offshore (Pakistan) but had BETTER overall language skills compared to Mint, and the ability to clearly answer questions AND correct issues. They even followed up by email to make sure I was happy. Yes, there were a few glitches, but both were fixed with ONE phone call each. AND you get your choice of either Verizon OR T-Mobile networks. If you're looking to save money with good service, US Mobile is the answer...

Okay, so I should say that if you live in a larger urban area your experience may be different, but I live in a smaller, rural town in NE Colorado.  I wanted to try Mint because I am a senior and working as a teacher, so as you can guess, don't have a lot of extra money.  I thought that switching from Verizon ($97/mo. with the teacher discount) to Mint ($15/mo.) was worth a try. BIG MISTAKE.  When I signed up, the person who I talked to assured me they had coverage in my area-that was my first question, so I signed up for the 3 month trial of $15/mo. -$45 upfront for unlimited data.  Good deal, right?  BUT Since I've been on MINT, I've had at best semi-functionality.  My family complains they can't get phone calls through to me and have to use FaceTime Audio.  When I make a phone call out, there is usually 1-2 minutes of dead air time before calls connect.  The worst is when I am in the car driving somewhere and I can't get GPS directions to load.  That happened recently on my way to the airport and it was scary because I was in highway traffic and didn't know when my turnoff was coming up.  Date functionality is fine as long as I'm at the house on my wirelesss network, but not while traveling/driving in NE CO.  Since I signed up, I have NEVER had any tower bars showing, they are all grey and just keep cycling, like the phone (an iPhone 11 Pro Max unlocked which should be compatible), is constantly searching for tower.  At first I thought this was just normal for MINT, but did some searching online and found nothing that suggested it.  I'm going on a 11-hour road trip in three days and so was starting to feel nervous this morning about leaving town.  What if my GPS won't load on the road and I don't know where I'm going?  What if I need to make a phone call and can't?  . . . I made 4 phone calls to MINT today, each one having to start at the beginning and explain myself  to people who I sort of understand.  Sorry, I KNOW that may sound anglocentric if not racist, but some of these people have very heavy accents. They all walked me through the same steps and nothing worked. Finally, the last call the tech I spoke to did some checking and found out that indeed, where I live Mint has "poor coverage" her words.  Had the first guy I spoke to at Mint been honest, I would not have made this mistake.  Since I've been with Mint a little over a week, no refund is possible.  So there goes $45.  Plus I paid de-activation fees on Verizon and am sure the reactivation fees will be costly too.  Like I tell my students, everything is a learning opportunity, this one was expensive.  I don't think MINT is too good to be true, and I'm sure for many, even most users, it works better.  But they should be very upfront and honest about their coverage area.  I don't live in the mountains.  I'm in a small rural community about an hour outside of Denver, and when I drive, I NEED to know I have have reliable service.  Granted, every service provider has coverage blank spots and can go out in remote areas, but I live in a town.

Needing to lower our phone costs, switching to Mint  seemed like a good idea.  It has turned out to be a nightmare.  I can only think that they assume everyone is an expert in the cell phone industry, because everything is self service.  I am not an expert, I did not understand what I needed to do, so I called and talked to a person.  I was told that it was a good thing, we were talking because some people have issues with their carriers, but he could help me get through all of that,  he told me that he had verified my phone was unlocked and I would have no problem getting switched over.  So, I paid my money, I got the sim cards in 2 days as promised.   I followed the "easy" instructions in the app, including cancelling my current service.  The activation did not happen, and there was nobody to help me last night,so I have been without a phone since dinnertime yesterday.  Today I have been on the phone and online for more than 4 hours trying to get my account activated, only to find out that the salesperson I had signed up with was "incorrect" and my phone is not unlocked.  One of the people at Mint that I talked to today assured me that she could get the phone unlocked, but ended up just hanging up on me,  I guess because it turns out she had lied to me, because the former carrier has refused to unlock the phone.  So, I have no phone service, and my phone number is in limbo.  They have told me they are refunding me, and that I can get another carrier with the same phone number, but we will see.  I would not recommend using this phone company, unless you are an expert in the field and are ok with spending hours online or on the phone.

I switched to Mint Mobile because I don't have the disposable income for the "big" provider's fees (AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile, all too expensive.) and yes, Ryan Reynolds was a draw, and Mint Mobile is supposed to use the T-Mobile network (which I don't have many complaints on coverage.)  First, they had me pay up-front for a SIM card, had a bit of trouble switching over because my number was on someone else's plan, but after a couple of hours with tech support, finally got it working.  Next day, I had a notice saying my service was up and I owed them money!  Had to pay TWICE to get my phone turned back on!  They refunded me the initial payment, but still.Now I'm having an issue with using Wi-Fi with calls and text.  ALL other services I've used automatically allow Wi-Fi calling/Text - just a matter of setting my phone to use Wi-Fi.  Mint?  You have to SET it to allow Wi-Fi Calling and Text, it comes disabled!  I set it, but I'm still having Text/Call issues over the Wi-Fi.Also, their customer support is less than desirable!  It's nearly impossible to find a Customer Service number on their website, and forget about finding it on their App!  When you call to "speak to a human being", you go through 5-minutes of automated menu systems before they put you in a waiting queue to speak to someone - IN INDIA!  Their heavy accents make it difficult to get things fixed.   I'm not in any way against them, they try their utmost best to help.  However, this is the second time within a month I needed to get a hold of someone because of issues with the service.  It's all I have to do my job and keep in touch with family/friends and it's failing most of the time.  NEVER had this problem with the big carriers.Mr. Reynolds, you might want to rethink attaching your name to this company!

Yes, it's great paying $15/month for each of my wife and my phone lines.  What's not great is that we repeatedly experience calls going straight to voicemail, many seconds of latency once the call is initiated, or at times finding that our Galaxy S21 phones that we purchased since our old phones were not compatible with Mint won't connect to the network so we can only make 911 calls.  I've spent more hours with tech support on so many occasions that they should be paying me.  We live in a major metro area, so coverage isn't the problem.  The typical fix is to reset the mobile network settings on the phone which is a little like patting your head and rubbing your stomach in order to make a phone call.  They escalate the problem, something is done, and the same thing happens again, and again.  Sometimes overtly setting the mobile carrier to Tmobile makes things work.  But the bottom line is that the service is not dependable.  Last week my wife and I were running errands.  I dropped her off and she was to call me with the when and where to pick her up.  Guess what!  Neither of our attempts to call each other worked.  This is nuts.  We're just about to throw in the towel on Mint.  This has been going on for several months now.  A service that is routinely not working is no service at all.  In fact it's dangerous.  Yeah, I like the cost, but maybe I am truly getting what I'm paying for in the deal.Update addressing Mint's reply:  The sage advice was to yet again call the same phone number that I've called so many times with the problem yet to be resolved by the Asian call center where understanding the person answering the phone presents an additional challenge.  In short, Mint's response was functionally irrelevant.  It might sound proactive but it's really a blowoff comment.

As far as I understand it, Mint Mobile is under the T-Mobile network. In fact, I think they technically own it, though they operate as a separate entity. So at a minimum, for the short and sweet of it, you know that you have a fairly large and reliable network on Mint.For years I have been on Verizon, my company paid for my cell phone. However, once I smelled that they were setting me up for outsourcing, I smelled it well in advance, I ported my number out.I ve been on my own plan for a few months, and Verizon is very expensive. They gave me a military discount for a while and some loyalty discounts; even with that, the cost of one month with Verizon exceeded the cost of 3 months with Mint.I should have switched months ago, but I didn t trust Mint enough yet. My wife pulled the trigger first and tried it as her AT&T bill was as bad as mine was, but she had no discounts. I helped her set it up, and she seemed satisfied with it after a few weeks.I made a last-ditch effort to save more money and stay on Verizon, but they couldn t drop it lower than 60+ dollars. Given the fact that I was being outsourced and losing my income, I too pulled the trigger and moved to mint.So after 3 months with them, I can t say that they are phenomenal, but I won t say they suck either. My area happens to have crappy service, and that was the case with Verizon and now with Mint. However, I did get a better signal on the subway with Verizon, though at times I get a better signal in my area with Mint. Give and take, crappy and decent signals in different areas.Overall, for my needs, Mint has been working out. Do note this though, if you are unsure of the plan you want, and don t pay on time, there is no grace period, those peeps shut you down on the spot. I can t use my phone right now and it s kind of annoying cause I don t have the card I d use on me, so I m basically dead in the water. You d think Ryan Reynolds would not nix our service that aggressively, but here we are.Oh, and they also charge you $8.50 to turn it back on. My wife didn t want me to write this because they are so cheap, but you should know about it. It s not a deal breaker, but be aware and don t slip on your renewals. Anyway that gripe aside, I will renew and stay on mint mobile for the time being. For what it is, it a really great deal.

I switched to Mint from Verizon 11 months ago, trying to save money by doing a 1 year prepaid plan. I did save money, at the cost of having absolutely terrible service. Not only is their cell tower system extremely limited outside of major cities, but they are also the the lowest priority on the cell system, because they contract out with other providers. So in high traffic situations they are the first provider to have service slowed or dropped all together.On top of that, I have had persistent issues using wifi calling and texting. It will completely stop working for no reason, with no warning. Even with high speed internet being broadcast over wifi at home, the ability to call or text over wifi would fail for me several times a week. When I reached out to customer service, they told me I was out of luck, and that it must be my phone, despite the fact that my phone was purchased from them, and had no issue connecting to wifi for normal internet/data access. I thought it might be a SIM card issue, but it would work fine sometimes, then completely fail other times. I would remove and reinsert the SIM card to see if that helped, and it didn't. My house is outside of the Mint cellular network, so my only option for calling or texting at home was via wifi. It seemed that when trying to call or text over wifi the phone still had to have some sort of connection to a cell signal, whether it was the Mint/T-mobile system, or via roaming from another service provider. Otherwise the SIM could not be verified, meaning I could not call or text at all. Completely unacceptable when they advertise on their website that wifi calling and texting was available even when not connected to a cellular system.So, I forfeited my last month of Mint prepaid to go back to Verizon. I will gladly pay 2-3x the price of mint to be on a service provider that actually has a signal at my house, at that doesn't lie about the services provided on their network.Being on Mint could literally cost you your life given its terrible signal coverage, and the wifi calling/texting not working for no apparent reason. Good luck getting an emergency call out over wifi if you experience some life threatening event.

Bring back the old international plan.  Your new Minternational Pass will break the bank for me. With your new plan, I will have to spend $10 each time I need to make a call on any particular day... or I could spend $120 and be able to make my 2 or 3 calls whenever I wanted... not very cost effective.  I'm also wondering if I would receive calls on days I didn't have a pass in effect.  If Mint will not bring the old plan back, I will be forced to look for another service.  Too bad, because I really foxing loved Mint Mobile.The new plan may be good for people who make a lot of calls every day, but it's terrible for those of us who make very few calls.  For us, the old plan was 5-star perfect.  Why not offer both?Addendum: Bring back the old international plan...Regarding the boilerplate replies from Mint Mobile that I've seen over and over on this site, they are not entirely identical as they do mention the specific issue that prompted their response.  Therefore, I can only assume that someone in Customer Support has already read the review and should be able to take whatever action deemed necessary to remedy the issue.  Thus the redundancy of asking the reviewer to call Customer Support seems like it would be a waste of both of our time.  Mint Mobile's decision to do away with the 'pay as you go', reasonably priced, no expiration international plans was a poor choice for those of us who use our phones infrequently.  Customer Support simply needs to make the people who can fix this, aware of the backlash this change has caused.

I thought,  Hey why not, sign me up! Goodbye Verizon after 8 years.  I thought I was such an informed super smart consumer.   But after almost 3 months of not being able to receive or make calls, or texts, unless I am standing outside in the Chicago winter, no Wi-fi calling even though the phone is capable of it, and absolutely no way to send or receive MMS, I am DONE.I logged almost eleven hours of customer (what a joke) service trying to get the service to work.   Finally, they kept transferring my call me up the food chain to an  IT specialist  that confessed that my phone was only  partially  compatible with Mint Mobile.    Partially compatible means for me I do not receive any calls while in the house.  Sometimes if I put the phone on the windowsill, I would get a text. Now mind you, I am talking about leaving a window cracked in Chicago in the winter.  Since my phone service does not work, I must have people email me on my laptop and tell me to call them.  I then must bundle up because it is friggin cold here in Chicago and go outside to have a phone conversation.   If someone did not know the  email me first to get a phone call from me  protocol, it would ring on their end and go to voicemail which of course I did not receive a notice about.I started the process of switching carriers to another carrier that uses VERIZON towers.   I was told by the new carrier that I need my Mint Mobile account number and PIN to port my number to them.  I thought NO problem, I will just log into my Mint Mobile account and get the information.  That opened another nightmarish can of worms.  If you go onto your account on the Mint Mobile s website and search everywhere, you will see that your own account number is NOWHERE to be found.   NO account number and NO PIN information.  I called them and I was on the phone for 36 minutes which started with the first retention person, and finally the 6th person my call was transferred to told me  Congratulations, you have completed the identity process, and we will now forward this to the porting team.   They should call you back in about a half an hour with your account number and PIN. I did not hold much faith in that, but it was a Christmas Miracle.  They did call me with my (secret) account number and PIN.Please, save yourself some time, aggravation, and heartache.  Pass up the Mint Mobile big   $15 a month  introductory offer up.
After stranding me overseas with no cell service WHICH I PAID FOR, they never fixed the problem, promised me they would reimburse me AND THEN FAILED TO DO SO.ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE COMPANY.

I just finished my 3-month trial with mint mobile and I am so glad that it is finally over. It was the worst service I have ever had. I work downtown and every single time I tried to make a phone call anywhere within a 15 mile radius of downtown, the call dropped not once or twice but constantly. It was beyond frustrating. Each call would take about 5 Seconds to connect. Texting was not immediate. Many times I didn't get a text until 10 minutes later. I contacted their customer service several times, utilizing both phone and chat. each time it was a chore just to get a person on the line. And then when I did, there was no customer service. All I received were canned responses about service outages and growing pains as a new startup. They did not offer any kind of credit for frustration, and basically acted as if I was an inconvenience. My last interaction was on chat and I told the representative my 3-month trial was up and I was hesitant to renew because of the many concerns I had... he response was "not his department" and before I could even type of single letter, I was in chat hold, out in cyberspace, without a single person checking on me for over 15 minutes. I told her that they just lost me as a customer, and what do you think happened? Right back into cyberspace. I think this company is all appearance. They have name recognition with a celebrity, a cute advertising campaign, a clever mascot, but that's all they are. Their coverage is terrible. According to the information I was given, there was supposed to be 100 percent coverage in the areas that I frequent, yet I had nothing but issues with trying to talk to people and even texting. My traveling during this 3-month period was strictly Urban areas. This is where you would expect the most coverage. I did not do any country or remote areas. Simply from City to city and downtown. I don't recommend them because of the terrible coverage which directly conflicts their own marketing, and also because of their lack of customer service.

I had a highly frustrating experience with Mint Mobile that I feel compelled to share. My encounter with this company left me deeply disappointed and questioning their commitment to customer satisfaction.My ordeal began when I mistakenly purchased hotspot data from Mint Mobile. Realizing my error almost immediately, I contacted their customer support to request a cancellation and refund. My intention had been to purchase international roaming credit, but the mix-up had me purchasing an unnecessary data plan. Given that I don't even use the regular 10GB of hotspot data that comes with my plan, it was clear that the purchase was entirely superfluous.To my astonishment, Mint Mobile refused to accommodate my request for a cancellation and refund. Keep in mind that this request was made within mere minutes of the mistaken purchase, and I had already explained my intention to purchase international roaming credit. This policy feels nothing short of predatory, especially for a company that markets itself as providing affordable phone options.Within just two days, I found myself out of $50 for international credit that was expected to be used for one, or at most two, phone calls. The fact that Mint Mobile would not rectify a purchase made in error reflects a complete lack of empathy and a disregard for customer satisfaction. It's as if they prioritize squeezing every last penny from their customers rather than fostering a positive and trustworthy relationship.I find it hard to reconcile this experience with Mint Mobile's claims of helping people by providing affordable phone options. If anything, my experience left me feeling scammed and taken advantage of. I can no longer justify continuing to use their services, and I would caution anyone considering Mint Mobile to think twice.I must commend the customer service representative I interacted with, Henry. He was patient and empathetic, doing his best to assist me within the confines of the company's policies. It's unfortunate that someone like Henry has to work for a company that seems more interested in profit margins than in serving its customers' needs. My experience with Mint Mobile was nothing short of disheartening. The refusal to accommodate a straightforward cancellation and refund request for a purchase made in error leaves me no choice but to discontinue their services. If you value fair and reasonable treatment as a customer, I urge you to think twice before considering Mint Mobile as your provider.

We go into phone shop thinking this will definitely fix it. We pay for a new line, apply the account number and transfer code .then the number will not transfer. It is not eligible for transfer apparently. By this time it is 8 at night. Another hour on the mint help line. Another supervisor. The shop has stayed open to help us out. Each call was a new negotiation. A nightmare.What will happen when my three month purchase gets near its end? Transferring numbers from Mint requires the lines to be active, so you can get an account number and transfer code. But they offer no accounts and no account numbers. It would be such a simple fix to make the service better.a) Offer a call back service so technical enquirers do not have to wait on the phoneb) List the account numbers and have a transfer code option on the website like all other carriers.c) Allow the help desk some sort of access to the people who actually control the lines, AND PLEASEd) Change the music every now and then. Mint Mobile's sign-up process is deceptive, and their follow-up even worse.They advertise EVERYWHERE "Unlimited everything for $15/month". Then they'll automatically place you in the lowest one that (in their estimation) you need. This is done in a way that's underhanded and entirely not obvious.When literal minutes later (in my case within the hour), you notice that you are not on the plan you thought you were signing up for, and call them, THEY REFUSE to make the correction on their end. This is what happened to me on July 22, 2023. Well, actually, they first refused (over chat). Then when I called they said they would make the adjustment. When a few days later nothing happened and I called back, they said that they "couldn't" make the change.This is, at best, a scummy business practice that is subjecting their customer support teams to who knows how many angry customer calls. At worst it's false advertising and a violation of fair trade laws.[EDIT] In response to Mint Mobile's comment response, I've contacted them via chat, twitter, and the number they provided multiple times. Should they wish to review my case they are welcome to connect with me via TrustPilot or the case number I shared in a previous edit of this review.[EDIT 2] I got the resolution I was looking for by making a BBB complaint. Neither Twitter nor Trustpilot were as quick.

I bought a Mint mobile plan for a second phone that I use strictly for work activity, and I keep all personal business on a different phone. As such, I feel comfortable connecting to the WiFi at both my office and home. I don t do much work (i.e., use data) when I m on the go and not connected to WiFi, but it does occur a bit. I also don t make a ton of calls or send and receive a ton of texts, but again, they do occur.So I got the 5GB/mo plan for $15/month, and it s been working out great.Pro tip: double-check in your settings to confirm which apps you really don t need access to while not connected to wifi, and go to cellular data and turn those off. You will still receive notifications for those apps, so if you really want to access the app while not on WiFi as a result of those notifications, then just go into cellular data and turn it back on for that app.Also look at background app refresh. That is for making sure the feed in the app keeps updates when you have it in the background. Turn that off for any app that requires you to log in with a password because that app will time you out anyway. Also turn off background refresh for all apps that you don t care about the feed not being updated if you re not actively using the app.For an app that has background refresh turned off, when you go back to that app, just refresh the app to see new items in the feed. (For iPhone, swipe down to refresh). Again, you ll still receive notifications for that app.The cellular coverage for Mint is  pretty good as far as I can see, and when my promo for free mobile phone service runs out with my current provider for my personal phone, I ll be trying out one month of unlimited with Mint Mobile for my personal phone, on which I use *far more* data (News, Amazon, WhatsApp, and of course navigation). I expect that it will be fine and I ll be able to save a TON over what I used to pay.My only gripe is that it s really hard to get a hold of a human. The in-app chat and the out-of-app texting are both horrible experiences. I waited 20 minutes in-app to chat with an agent, gave up, and went to the out-of-app texting because I wasn t sure what was going on. The app doesn t tell you if it will notify you if you have the app in the background or the phone screen off/locked, so that s the first thing.But worse is that they say they ll connect you as soon as they can but never give any updates.After I gave up on the in-app chat, I switched to out-of-app texting (+1 (800) 683-7392). I waited FORTY MINUTES there for an agent. That is totally ridiculous, but again, even worse is that they do not give you any updates about your place in line or how much longer they think you re going to need to wait. That is upsetting because it makes me wonder if there was a connection problem and I m waiting around for nothing.So after another 20 minutes of waiting, I called in. It took two minutes of pressing buttons to get into the line to wait for a human. Once I was in that line, the connection to speak to a human was very quick. I don t remember how long it took or if it was instantaneous.The person who helped me did a great job. They were extremely helpful and patient and explained everything very well. I was very satisfied with the result.Later, I received a text message notification from the out-of-app text messaging from an agent who was ready to help me via text. I let the person know my problem had already been resolved and asked them for an email to Customer service. They said they did not have an email to give me and that instead, I should call the number again and give them relate my experience to them.I don t feel like waiting anymore or pushing any more buttons and waiting again, so I will just leave my review here.Until this experience, I would have said mint Mobile was four stars.However, due to all the waiting around in the texting and chat service with zero updates in the app or via text message once the waiting began, I had to take off one star.Plus, their chat bot is pretty useless. It only provides extremely basic information.Bottom line: they re pretty good, but if you want to get a hold of somebody in customer service, do not bother using any chat feature with them. Instead, call in and press 4.

How bad things have gotten with Mint  VERY.I don t want to have to pay 1 year in advance to get a $15 rate for calls and texts only for their eSIM service.  I only use wifi calling as I need this to speak with friends, family and colleagues in the USA.After taking DAYS to get a response on webchat, I finally managed to get my account number and porting PIN so I could move over to my new service provider Tello.comThey are charging me $8/month for unlimited calls and texts on a MONTHLY basis (not yearly like Mint do).Sadly, due to Mints ineptitude, I had to  renew  for three months to get my Port Out authorised (yes, Im a brit, yes with spell it with an S :-) )Im now working with my bank to recover these funds as this issue was caused by Mint.If you do look to port out here are my tips 1 - Start the port out process at least TWO WEEKS before your end date.2 - You will need to use Webchat (they no longer want to talk to customers via email) - be prepared that you will be on hold and transferred around a LOT.  When you do get to the right person, they will text you a one-time passcode.3 - They will give you a long account number and a PIN Number, you need BOTH to be able to port out.4 - Give these to your NEW provider in the format / way they need it.5 - It can take anywhere between a couple of minutes and 2 business days for this part to work.Once the number has moved, it can take a few more days for the Caller ID and CNAM to work properly on the new network.I hope by reading this rather long review you will see there is an alternative and you can the fox off Mint Mobile.

Had Mint Mobile for almost a year and the coverage was relatively good in larger cities but did not have any in smaller cities / towns.
