Ultimately decided to switch to another carrier to get better coverage and upgrade to a new phone without paying for the entire cost immediately.For those looking to make any cancellations, read the fine print thoroughly. We cancelled under the assumption that we could recoup the unused data from our automatic 3-month payment (had about 1-2 months worth of data unused). Come to find out that they will not refund any terminations past the 45 days upon the purchase even though you're paying for it monthly, quarterly, or annually depending on the plan you choose.Ultimately, you get what you pay for. Price is hard to beat, service / coverage is mediocre. Customer support is great / easy to get ahold of. Refund policy is garbage.

I want to warn anyone considering switching to Mint Mobile: DO NOT purchase any more than one month at a time for multiple months, even if purchasing more months at a time will save you money in the long-run. If you have issues with service after 45 days of activating a service, they WILL NOT refund you any money even if you can't use their service.We had adequate service for the first month, so we decided to buy a 12-month plan after that to save money in the long-run (to get the advertised deal of $15 per month per line). At about half-way into the next month, none of our phones would work consistently at the same address. We reached out to customer support 3 times to get the issue fixed and none of their recommendations/troubleshooting fixed the issue. The last customer support person just recommended we wait it out to see when we would get service back, which is a horrible idea to tell people who need regular cell phone contact for appointments.We purchased 3 lines at over $600 for a 12-month plan, having only used half of a month of service, then we decided to switch due to being without cell service at our address for over 2 weeks. When I requested the refund, they denied it and wouldn't refund ANY of the money, so we had to switch carriers and being out of over $500 in unusable service. The email denial I got simply stated their policy:"You are not eligible to request a refund for a new Mint Mobile service activation or SIM Kit when forty-five (45) calendar days have passed since the date of purchase ( 45-Day Refund Exclusion )."I would advise to pay month-by-month and not expect any refunds back after 45 days regardless of the reason. They are unwilling to work with you after that point. I hope this review helps others to refrain from trusting Mint Mobile until you have had months into service.

Now I am 2 hours into talking with customer service, finally they agreed to refund the 82 $ to my bank account. This however would take 7 to 10 business days. I have no option but to use Mint, pay the hidden fee, or not have phone service for two weeks while they refunded the money to my bank account.Avoid mint mobile at all costs, they practice deceptive advertising on all levels as a standard business practice. Not one piece of honesty or respect for the customer. I am very dissatisfied and will be contacting a class action lawyer, or taking them to small claims for the $5 because of how they handled this situation. I don't care how much time it takes at this point, this was the worse customer service I've ever had. Again, avoid at all costs.Q: Why not include in the marketing materials and advertisements that Mint charges hidden fees?A: To bait the customer into thinking the price was $15, and then to switch it to $17.50 after the customer makes their purchasing decision - which is the definition of "bait and switch" marketing. 90% of customers will go ahead and pay this fee, so they build this into their business practice, because if they were honest up front about the price, customers wouldn't have pursued using their service in the first place, thus stealing time and lying to their customers.

Mint mobile is a great wireless company and they provide 4 plans; 5GB, 10GB, 15GB, and Unlimited. For context, most people use less than 10GB of mobile data per month.Mint's plans cost $15, $20, $25, and $30 for their respective plans. These are record low prices in the US wireless industry, with ATT and Verizon charging a whopping $55 to $90 per month for their unlimited plans.I've been with Mint Mobile for more than a few months (I've known about Mint earlier; I'm a tech blogger), and the most important thing that any carrier provides is coverage.Coverage is pretty good with Mint Mobile. Although rural areas may lack fast internet, in most urban and suburban areas, the network is excellent. Coverage is great, and I had great internet on a 4hr drive through the heart of Georgia, USA.In my opinion, customer service is also an important factor of any wireless company. Mint Mobile has an average customer service team, but I believe this is where MVNO's, or cheap wireless providers, all lack on. Their team is best at answering normal questions, but not so great at problem-solving. I would like to see improvements in the support team.A lot of people heard of 4G LTE and 5G in advertisements. 4G is the older, and slower network most people use today. Speeds with 4G can range from a slow and laggy 5 mbps to a decently fast 70 mbps or above in areas.5G, on the other hand, should provide much faster speeds, so you can watch movies and game on the go. 5G is rolling out in most urban and suburban areas with speeds from a 40 mbps to upwards of 500 mbps.Mint Mobile runs on the T-Mobile network (they don't have their own), and T-Mobile is known for speed. T-Mobile has both the largest and the fastest 5G network in the US, with over 90% of americans, both rural and urban, are covered with 5G. Mint provides a fast and similar experience to T-Mobile.Now lets talk about extra features. Mint Mobile has free calling to the US, Canada, and Mexico, and free texting anywhere in the world! Hotspot uses data from your plan, unless you are with an unlimited plan, where you have a seperate allotment.International Roaming through Mint Mobile is an option, although the price set for it would discourage most roamers. Instead, if you have an E-Sim compatible device (check online), you can buy an Airalo data plan worldwide for low rates.Mint Mobile is an excellent wireless company, but could work on some aspects of it's customer service. Break away from long and expensive contracts with Mint Mobile (not sponsored) and pay much less for the necessary data. So Mint is definetely worth it!

After being transfered back to customer service I am told there is a glitch in the system that is preventing me from being able to be transferred to a supervisor... ... ... ... ... ... ...Its Saturday night so I will either get the issue fixed with MINT in the next few hours or tomorrow I'll be heading back to TMOBILE with hat in hand begging them to take me back. I'm getting good (OK bad) experience with cell phone providers since Airvoice began having billing issues.Mint showed I had coverage, so I gave them a try on two lines that had both been working fine with Airvoice (AT&T MVNO).The first drawback is you have to buy 3 months of service at a time. After that, to keep the same monthly cost you have to buy a whole year of service. They also sock it to you with taxes and various account fees, so it's really NOT $15/month.It took several days and me (not Mint as they advertise) calling Airvoice to get both numbers transferred. One somehow had a "network sim lock" and I had to pay a third party $20 to get past that. I'm presuming this had to do with the phone coming from an AT&T MVNO to a T-Mobile one.When my first number finally transferred, about 3 days after the sims arrived, I was happy.. until I tried to make a call. Using a network signal strength app, I found the only place I had anywhere close to a usable signal was near the phone signal booster I use. When I did make a call, the person on the other end couldn't hear me. I called a phone that has a Wi-Fi number and verified the Mint call was illegible- a lot of static and dropouts.Long story short, if you have good coverage with AT&T, you may not with T-Mobile. Fortunately, I was within the 7 days for refund & have filed for a refund.UPDATE: Lowered to 1 star after spending an hour + on chat to try to get account # and PIN for transfer. Finally got it for one number, still going thru hoops via chat.Suggestion: Users should be able to get this info after logging in. I can set up service and make a payment online, why can't I get this info?

Upon my return finally found another minimal English speaking helpless desk person, who after being threatened with just cancelling my subscription, refunded part of the money Cosmote had defrauded me of. I am now searching for alternatives to Mint and will never refer another person to Mint, in the future. Sad that a relatively good vendor should decline so far in such a short period of time.

They actually don't have international data. I'm in Mexico City with my six month Mint Mobile plan and after eight frustrating calls (two were dropped and no one called back even though they asked for my phone number)to customer service before I gave up. To improve first off they need to hire CSRs that can speak English. They may think they are speaking English but take it from a native born English speaker thier conversations are barely understandable. Then there is the background noise; pots and pans clanging,people shouting, children screaming, even if they were speaking English you couldn't hear them.Here's the story, I bought my SIM at Best Buy back home in Michigan, went home and installed the card in my blu J9L dual SIM phone (which I have used in Europe with SIMs from different carriers) turned on the phone and...no connection. I called customer service,from a different phone, and was told in barely understandable english that the problem was my phone was locked to T- Mobile and so the SIM wouldn't work in my phone. I explained that was not true as I have used many different SIMS in my phone. She then told me I needed to install a new VPN on my phone so it would recognize Mint Mobile. Not true, my settings showed the phone was set to the Mint Mobile VPN. At this point the background noise was so bad I couldn't hear her so I hung up. I spent about an hour tweaking various settings and finally got voice and data working. Problem solved... until I arrived in Mexico. Voice service worked but not data. I called customer service again and they informed me that even though I paid for international credits I didn't purchase the international data (I didn't see any thing about this on the website or online. So I purchase the International data and...no data. So another five calls to customer service. Twice I was disconnected and the rest of the calls were a series of being instructed to reset various things and restart my phone. Nothing worked and the calls were excruciating. The last call the CSR told me that it was probably a bad cell tower and she could try and reset the service if I could tell her where exactly in the city I was. I had been and would continue to be moving all over the area and data didn't work anywhere so I figured that was grasping at straws in bad English with loud background noises.So I took the Mint Mobile SIM out of my phone and tossed it. I went to the nearest Mexican convenience store and purchased Mexican TelCel SIM. The clerk didn't speak any English(I speak very little Spanish) but I could understand him better than the Mint Mobile CSRs and we quickly popped the TelCel SIM in and it worked perfectly from the get go. The TelCel SIM was $20.00 US with 3 GB of data. I can top off on line or just about any convenience store in Mexico. I blew $90 all told on Mint Mobile. For just a second I considered taking legal action, not for the $90 but the mental anguish. Today I met some folks from the US who had the same issue, no data on their Mint Mobile SIM and it was never resolved. So I have concluded there is no international data with Mint Mobile. It's all a sham.

Here is a summary of my experience using Mint Mobile for 9 months Feb to Nov:During the 7-day trial period it was simply amazing!  For the first time ever I experienced 5G speeds.  For the entire 7 days, no matter what time of the day, it was simply perfect.  Wow, what an impression!  Sign me up. I did the one year prepay and also my wife too.So what happened?  Why the one star rating?  Well, literally the first day after the 7 day trial, we no longer were getting the 5G data, it switched to LTE.  No problem, it was sufficient for our needs  . When it worked that is, which was practically never.  It got worse as time went on.  To the point of useless.In the fine print Mint mentions the possibility of being deprioritized by the host network which is T-Mobile.  That needs to be taken out of the fine print and made into a Bold statement to say: for being dumb to prepay with a no-name cell provider that has to borrow from a competitor major carrier network, you are guaranteed to be left with the data crumbs of no connection or connection speeds of 0.01Mbps.Think about that.  Mint has to rely on a competitor for network access.  That is bound to fail and we experienced it.We stuck it out for awhile, but there was just zero data availability.  Especially when we were connected to 5G with a solid connection.  I d contact Mint tech support asking what s going on, and they d refer to their famous coverage map (which is just the same T-Mobile map but colored green) that shows excellent coverage, but what I learned, the hard way, is all about deprioritization.Basically, Mint users such as my wife and I, were permanently being deprioritized by T-Mobile.  T-Mobile will take care of their customers first, of course, so in a rapidly growing area like south Florida, Mint users like us simply are left unconnected.Numerous attempts to try to get a workaround from Mint Mobile were just a waste of time.  They re powerless to get T-Mobile to relax their deprioritization.  Finally we had enough.  We still have three months left with Mint but today we re going to get changed over to T-Mobile like we should have done in the first place.  What we thought was going to be massive savings has turned out to be a total loss.  Thankfully it s just a few hundred dollars.The business model for these mobile virtual network operators (MVNO) is very risky.  We learned our lesson.If you never need data, then Mint is for you.  Voice and text messages did work ok, but for texts, don t try to send files like a photo because it won t send.In summary, you get what you pay for.

I called Mint with a list of questions just to make sure I knew what to expect and I made sure my iphone13 was unlocked and ready to go.  So I made the move  .. and thus began the biggest nightmare I have experienced in my cell phone history! Yep, I know, a first world problem  . Until you are stuck in LA in the middle of the night without access to maps, or the AAA, then it s still a first world problem but with a bit more at stake.I transferred my account, using my  transfer pin number  from my now ex carrier which enables me to keep my number of 10yrs. I was told that there would be no problem and at the most I would be off the grid for a couple of hours. I then received a flurry of emails welcoming me to the  family  and informing me that my plan and phone were now active! Only problem was, someone had forgot to tell my phone, it wasn t  active  and it wasn t connected to the super reliable Mint network.I have so far spent a total of 7hrs (I am currently online again, in my 8th hour with my 5th  specialist ). This has been going on for 2 days now and of those 5 specialists, 2 PROMISED to call back after they had spoken with someone in technical   they never did   1 specialist actually hung up on me when it all just got too difficult for her, and the other 2 at least were honest enough to say they really didn t know how to solve the problem, they didn t have the necessary training. The  specialist  I am currently working with has just told me that he just rang my number and it was working fine, I told him to check the number because my phone never rang. He did so, and yep, he was calling the wrong number!As a result, I can t just cancel my plan with mint and go back to my old carrier with my same number because in order to get back my  transfer pin number  the line has to be validated which means up and running with Mint. So here I am stuck in some kind of limbo land.I know that their reply to this review will be the usual passive, placating  we ll always endeavor to fix the  problem  kinda response. Don t believe them. Their customer service people actually lied to me on three occasions, just to get the sale.And you, Ryan Reynolds, as the bold face of Mint, your company sucks, and you can no longer be taken at your word. God knows what you put in your Gin.Go with these cowboys at your own peril and good luck down their rabbit hole of lies.Ps I have just been told by this fifth  specialist , after an hour and a half online with him that he doesn t know how to fix the problem or what to do  .. at least he is honest. So I am now paying Mint for an account that I can t use.Ignore the one star, they don t even rate that.

In late July, 2022, I thought I'd give Mint Mobile a try, so sent for a trial SIM which came very quickly.  I installed it into a new, unlocked Moto G Stylus 2022 (non-5G), then installed their Android app and activated the card.  I then ported over my old number, which happened in a matter of minutes as verified through my phone's settings.  That's where the good stuff ended.A couple of days later, I got an e-mail from them telling me that the porting of my old number was now complete.  Uh-oh; that was completed a while ago.  So, I checked my phone's settings again and, sure enough, the phone now showed the temp number they had originally given me.  I now couldn't make or receive calls.This began a series of six calls to tech support, beginning on Aug 3, 2022 and ending (so far) on Aug 23.  In each call, I was assured that the problem was or soon would be fixed.  What they did fix was my ability to make and receive calls at the correct number, but not so that it would be correct within the phone itself.  This is a problem, in part, because some services, like Telegram, rely on that number for proper function.I was told on three occasions that this was definitely a mistake on their part, that the issue would be "expedited," and that I would receive a notification once it was fixed.  I NEVER heard from them, and the problem remains.If you look at the other reviews, here, you'll see loads of Mint's cut 'n' pasted, standard reply asking you to give support a call.  Judging from my several experiences, I am convinced that doing so is nothing more than a waste of time.  They should provide a name and number to someone who can actually fix the problem, rather than those who are apparently trained to simply put you off until you go away - although that strategy seems to be working famously.Lastly, and I truly don't mean to offend anyone for this is simply a practical matter, if you happen to be a native English speaker, be prepared for a serious linguistic challenge.  I was raised in an ethnically diverse city but could barely understand several of their staff.I wanted to like them, but their demonstrable incompetence has ensured not only my never recommending them, but also my cautioning anyone who might even be considering them.

If you don't read my entire lengthy disaster story below, just believe that all these other horror stories are true.  Save yourself the grief & avoid Mint Mobile!!I was looking for a reasonably priced carrier that would accept my two year old phone & allow me to transfer over my phone number I've had for over 22 years.  That's where I found Mint Mobile...& I wished I hadn't.I received my SIM card on a Wednesday & at 3:45 pm, excitedly put it in my phone to start the process.  It seemed rather simple.  Though, transferring my number it said could take time (up to 48 hours) & it asked if I wanted to use my old carrier until they could or get assigned a temporary new number through Mint.  I selected to use my old carrier.  But, somehow it still went through in proceeding with a temporary number through Mint...hmm.  I then got a notification about a minute later that my number had been successfully transferred...hmm again, so much for up to 48 hours.  That's where the real issues started.  It said I didn't have service...by the "X" where my signal bar indicator used to be.  At times the 5 bars would show back up, but none of them were lit (indicating any type of signal).  I had a voicemail notification (I'm guessing to set-up my new voicemail through Mint).  When trying to call out to it, it would either tell me I wasn't on a registered network or the call would appear to start, but just keep circulating & never go through.  I restarted the phone & T-Mobile popped up as the carrier, so I knew the new SIM had successfully installed.  I chatted with a Mint rep (since I couldn't call for help!) & they went through several troubleshooting items...none of which worked.  A couple times, they told me it was because my phone was locked.  My phone is not locked as it was paid off 1.5 years ago & has been used at two different carriers already without issue.  He then offered to mail my a new SIM...which would take a couple of days.  Knowing it wasn't the SIM, & I work from home (& need a working phone!), I just decided to cancel the process & stay with my current carrier.  He sent me a link for a refund - which I signed up for but still haven't gotten confirmation of (reading the other reviews, I am not hopeful so I have a dispute with my credit card company on st&by!).But my issues didn't stop there, no.  I then popped my old SIM card through US Cellular (my prior carrier) back in a voila!  It still worked with my old number & I immediately began receiving text messages I had been missing during this two-hour disaster that was having the Mint Mobile SIM card in!  I then called US Cellular to make sure my number transfer wasn't going to go through...they told me it was pending, but the fact I was talking to them from my old number through their service was odd, but a good indication it could be stopped.  However, that had to be done through Mint Mobile...great.  So I called them & that rep said he couldn't stop it as the number transfer already went through at 1:25 PM...hmm, even with the time zone difference I didn't even put the SIM card in at that time? He then warned that because of that, our phone call could cut out at any point (great excuse to hang up on me when he can't resolve my issue!).He then told me he couldn't provide me any details on how to transfer my number back until the refund was processed...huh?!  He then said if I wanted to stay, I could always make calls through wifi...double huh?!  Why signup with a cell phone carrier only to have to make calls when you have access to wifi?!18 hours later & I have yet to see a refund, & my phone number is still working through my prior carrier.  Though, I am still a little leery that it will suddenly cut out.  However, given the facts & other reviews, I believe they never successfully transferred it to begin with.  They clearly have a lot of kinks to work out so just save yourself the grief & look elsewhere!  This is not a carrier I have any confidence in.8/25/22 Update: 27 hours later, I got an email my refund was approved- progress!  It has yet to hit my back account yet, but I'm hopeful as I also got a notification from PayPal.  However, more frustration started about 30 minutes later when my cell phone number, which was still working on the sim card through my prior carrier stopped working.  I couldn't call or text so I assumed that meant Mint completely shut down my cell phone number...even though it was still tied to my US Cellular sim card.  So, I got on wifi to chat with Mint since I again couldn't call out.  She then asked me for account information & said for the final verification, she would send me a text message & I need to provide that code to her - uh, you shut down my number how am I suppose to get a text?!  She then said I could instead provide the last 3 numbers I called - uh, I could never get a signal with Mint to do so!  Finally, after an hour, they provided me the info to get my original number back!

First, I give them a  1/2 reliability mark -- a few dropped calls & occasional poor signal, which is similar to their network carrier T-Mobile but good enough. Now the bad. After an overall acceptable 3-month trial, I decided to purchase a 12-month plan to keep the cost down. I paid for it using my Mint Mobile account on 8/16/22, four days before the trial plan expired. On 8/20, my new plan was set to restart but did not. I opened a chat session and was told my new plan would activate at 2:27pm PT. Still no refresh later on, so I opened a new chat session where the agent had to escalate to 2 upper technicians. Two attempts to refresh failed, but on the third try, my service was canceled -- no phone, no text, account expired -- leaving me hanging with no service that was prepaid for a year. Fortunately I still had an active chat session on my PC and asked the agent why they canceled my service. After about an hour wait time, I was told my credit card company issued a charge back, and that I would need to redo the payment (END DAY 1). The next morning, I checked my credit card transaction log to confirm the Mint Mobile charge posted on 8/17. I then opened a third chat session to contest their explanation, but a supervisor was steadfast it was an error on my side. So I called my credit card company, their CSR verified they did not issue a charge back & I opened a dispute to get my 12-month charge refunded. I have a transaction history from both sides, so I will win the dispute, but I lost cell service for at least several days, they lost a customer for life, and I will be moving to a more reliable wireless carrier. Bottom line: Competitive pricing when you commit to a long-term one-year plan if you can actually get it, but ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE CUSTOMER SERVICE. AVOID LIKE COVID UNLESS YOU LIKE TO CHAT FOR HOURS AND WALK AWAY EMPTY HANDED.

I was a longtime Verizon user and didn t pay much attention to phone bills in the past. I don t think I will keep renewing for a few reasons.First and foremost, the headline fee is very attractive. $120 for 6mo 4G plan. $150 for 6mo 10G plan. But there s a catch. There s a $11 fees and taxes on top of the 4G plan. This is not the problem. The real problem is, if you choose to upgrade to the 10G plan, there is $22 tax and fees on top of the plan, while the incremental charge on the plan is just $30. So in other words, on paper they say $120 for 4G plan and $150 for 10G plan, in reality the 10G plan is more than $40 more expensive than the 4G plan. Mint doesn t give you a statement or itemize those expenses for you. So this is what happened to me - i thought i might try the 10G and pay for the $30 difference and in the end was charged $42 and tried four different customer service reps and no one could explain the disproportionate taxes and fees.That s probably my biggest negative surprise. And the Mint app and customer service line made it ridiculously easy to make payments and completely the opposite when you have dispute.In addition, I rarely used more than 4G when I was working in the office full time and using Verizon. But when switched to Mint, I used up the 4G data 3 out of the 6 months there - and I was only going into the office 2 days a week (when I m at home I use the home wifi).And lastly, the customer service was really in line with the price you pay for your plan. So I went in knowing that I get what I paid for. Maybe Verizon had poor customer service as well but for the 10yrs I was with them I never had to call them.
Just stay away. Gonna launch mediator case, because extra money they have charged 2 times, they do not reply to any mails.

Live in a city,and sometimes voice service is very good, but data can be non-existent.  Did some research and found the following:First, like unlimited plans for practically all carriers, it's not unlimited in the most straightforward sense of the word. Mint deprioritizes your data after 35GB has been used up, meaning that you'll still get service, but speeds will slow. Typically, carriers try to dance around this with jargon, but Mint's site paints it clear as day, stating "customers using >35GB/mo will experience lower speeds."What's more, unlimited customers have their video streams capped at standard-definition, 480p quality; this limitation doesn't exist for 3GB, 8GB or 12GB users. Unlimited subscribers also don't get unlimited hotspot usage, as that runs out after just 5GB. Therefore, if feeding your phone's data connection to your laptop, tablet or another device is very important to you, you're better off going with the 12GB-per-month tiered plan.It's also worth noting that once you pass your monthly data allotment for a tiered plan, you're throttled to 2G speeds.Mint has gotten favorable feedback for its inexpensive rates and simple user experience. However, if you have to call up customer service, the experience may be less than ideal. Subscribers on the Mint Mobile subreddit have cited poor support, especially compared to the Google Fi network, which prides itself on speedy replies and availability through multiple social channels. To make matters worse, the lack of a brick-and-mortar presence means Mint customers have fewer resources at their disposal, should something go wrong.As far as network performance is concerned, users have cited slower speeds, on average, for Mint Mobile when compared to T-Mobile. This likely has to do with Mint's customers being deprioritized in favor of T-Mobile's own base. That's not a unique phenomenon for prepaid users, who almost always end up playing second fiddle to postpaid subscribers. However, some Mint customers say they've reached peak speeds from time to time, possibly when network conditions were less congested.So it seems that the issue is as much as T-Mobile, maybe more than than Mint.

Well, my review is going to mirror many others that I have read here.  I guess I should have placed more weight on those reviews but like many, I got sucked into Mint service by their great prices and the deal they were offering at the time of my signup.  Mint reminded me of how important good customer service is, especially with a cell provider.  I thought ATT had the worst customer service ever but Mint has clearly grabbed that trophy and jumped way out into first place.Quite unbelievable what I went through with them and I consider myself an easy customer to please.  In a nutshell, these fools inadvertently shut down my service after two days and claimed I was canceled due to lack of payment!!  I had signed up for the 3+3 deal they were offering and paid the $240 up front so there was no basis for the cancelation.My initial order was for 4 accounts from Mint as I wanted to switch my parent's phones along with mine and my wife's to Mint but ATT would not unlock my parent's flip phones (another unbelievable story) so I never could initiate service with Mint.  At that point, I asked Mint to refund my parent's accounts. Apparently Mint has no ability to issue a partial credit.  Found out the cause of the service interruption was in their attempt to credit my parent's order initial order, they inadvertently credited all accounts, thus canceling my service on all accounts.   On top of that, once our 'new' service was shut off, I had to chat in to see what the problem was and was informed by some rude CS agent that the problem was mine and that I needed to pay another $120 to turn my service back on!  Additionally, he told me that because a refund was issued, that I could no longer get the 3 + 3 deal and now my cost would be the full price!  Basically, they shook me down for another $120 (after I paid $240 a couple of days prior to start the service) and the service still didn't work.  The next day after giving them 24 hours to reinstate my service I returned to the chat line and was told there was an issue with my account, that it would be escalated and they would get back to me in a few days.  Unbelievable.  They now held $360 of mine and I had no phone service and no estimate of when it would reinstate.The next day I moved my service over to another provider and requested a full refund based on Mint's "full no questions asked within 7 days refund ".  I was told the only option to get a refund was to Paypal or be mailed a check.  I chose the check which they said would arrive in 7-10 days.  That was over 3 weeks ago and after chatting in with several agents since, I was informed today that the issue has been 'escalated' so I can get the credit that I was promised 3 weeks ago.I saw another review where someone requested "Overnight Shipping" of the SIM cards.  When I started the service with Mint, I requested the same and paid the $15 additional charge to ship 'Overnight'.  'Overnight' to Mint means 5-7 days although they are less than clear about that.  I asked a rep when my SIMs would ship and he said because I selected "Overnight" shipping, I would have mine the very next day.  Wrong.  They require 2-3 days just to process the order so 'Overnight' is incredibly misleading.  I called in to verify and was told they were sorry for not being clear and that my shipping costs would be refunded.  Wrong again.  They don't refund shipping even if they say they will.Bottom line: Trying Mint Mobile was hands-down the worst experience I have ever had with any company.  In fact, it's still ongoing as I have not received my refund after 3 weeks and talking to dozens of people, all who told me contradictory information.Dealing with them was a true clown experience and each and every time I chatted or called in, I had to repeat my entire story.  I even saved all chat transcripts and showed those to the agents I was speaking with.  It did not matter one bit as they would just contradict what was written in the transcript.  Trying to reach anyone of authority is futile as well as no managers or supervisors are available and the one time I was able to get one (after being on the phone for 90 minutes with them) he was a complete jerk who basically told me there was nothing he could do and all I could do was wait until they figure their stuff out.I have never written a bad review about any company online but this experience was like no other.  I am compelled to share my experience with others with the hope that they can possibly avoid the same.  Please spare yourself the grief and don't buy into the promise of 'great service at a great price' from these folks. It's all fake.I see all the Bot responses to bad reviews telling people to reach out to customer service.  You're wasting your time, folks.  Don't bother with these guys as there are plenty of other providers who do a great job.

Overall unreliable. I used Mint Mobile for the better part of a year, since March 2021. I moved cities in that time and after I moved is when Mint's service really took a dive. For context, Mint's coverage map claims that both cities have excellent coverage.Immediately after my move, my phone service just sort of faltered and stopped one day. That means no texts, calls, or data to use. Of course, luck would have it that I discovered I didn't have service whilst I was stranded on the side of the road, in need of a tow truck. Initially, I was told there was an outage in our area, their engineers were working on it, please wait patiently. Customer service was not able to provide an ETA on when it might  be working again or what caused the outage. The problem persisted for three weeks and had to be resolved by sending me a new SIM card.New SIM card worked for a few weeks, then service one day just sort of faltered and quit again. Tried all of the same troubleshooting as the first time, including a new SIM card, but nothing worked. Customer service reps even "escalated my case" twice with instructions that if it wasn't fixed in 24 hours to contact them again. Neither escalation fixed anything. I'm being told that it's another outage and that they don't know when it will be fixed.The final straw was while I was awaiting COVID results. I could not go into work until I was proven negative. I really needed this income. In order to see my COVID results, I had to create an account with the company who tested me and view the results online. However, in order to view my results, I had to put in a confirmation code....that was texted to me. Because I couldn't send or receive texts, I wasn't able to login to my results which caused me to miss a few extra days of work since I did end up testing negative for COVID. Again, that was income I desperately need. I still currently don't have any bars, whatsoever.I posted a review on the Mint Mobile subreddit, but it was taken down since it wasn't glowing. When I spoke with an moderator about my post, they were apathetic and unreasonable. Eventually, the admin got frustrated and banned me. I'm not sure how involved Mint Mobile is with their subreddit, specifically if they pick their moderators. But it puts a bad taste in my mouth. Why censor an honest review?In the three months since I've moved, I have spent a cumulative 6 weeks without service. What am I paying for if there's only a 50% chance my data is going to work on any given day? Aside from the tow truck and COVID disruptions that my lack of cell service caused me, there were a couple other safety issues that came up where I had no way to contact friends or the police in an emergency. Living alone in a new city, I learned the hard way that it's imperative to have reliable cell service not just for modern conveniences but for safety. If you and your family are looking for something reliable, I would skip Mint Mobile. Customer service is always friendly and earnestly tries to help, but ultimately you'll be paying for a service that only partially works. Hopefully I can get my money back for remaining two months of data I bought.I'd like to close by saying, please remember to be kind to your Mint customer service representatives. It's not their fault your services are not working in the first place. They are aggressively gracious if you're respectful.Update: I renewed my subscription for another 3 months and only used a month before switching. In that month, I had literally 0 data. I asked for my money back and they said per their rules, I do not qualify for a refund. So I practically gave them $120 for nothing in return. When I went to switch my phone to a different service, it turned my phone into a brick. Something about a code I needed that is impossible to find. I ve seen others with the same issue. Customer service was no help. I ended up having to get an entirely new phone with my parents help. If I weren t lucky to have my parents  assistance, I would have had NO phone. Literally lighting my $120 on fire would have been more helpful than giving it to Mint Mobile as I at least could have had heat and some s mores.

I switched four phones to Mint mobile when we moved to the USA. All four phones (iphone and Samsung) were purchased in the USA as well. From the onset we found one bar as a common status and often, no bars at all. It seemed like coverage was totally random. In one case, we went from full bars to no bars while sitting in the same location. We restarted our phones and still had zero bars. An hour later we had full bars, then back to zero. This was all in one location and on all four phones.This level of service was is in Washington DC, Northern Virginia and Baltimore and we expected great coverage in these areas. Customer service was no help. They had us reset our phones, do the chip reset, and fiddle with settings, but in the end, the problem is with Mint and not anything with our phones.After two weeks, as a test, I got a chip from T-Mobile. We now have max bars, no dropped service and no dropped calls. We are now converting all of our phones to T-Mobile but it could have been AT&T or Verizon or basically anyone else I the DC area. It is a area with plentiful coverage.Saving money with Mint would have been great but we also need reliable service for our family. Mint didn't deliver any form of reliability. If there is any bright side, we only prepaid a few hundred dollars and while we know that money was wasted, it could have been worse.
